title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. The move reflects growing confidence in the U.S. aluminum market under new trade policies.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: A new energy-hungry aluminum smelter, set to be America's first since 1980, is awaiting a final power agreement. Once operational, it will more than double the nation's primary aluminum production capacity.

title: NEW: Trump's tariffs credited for this MAJOR boost to US aluminum production
summary: Fox News reports that Trump's tariffs have directly contributed to Century Aluminum's decision to increase production. The policy shift is seen as a catalyst for revitalizing U.S. aluminum manufacturing.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers have developed a new nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from manufacturing waste streams, allowing the metal to be upcycled and reducing hazardous waste. The membrane selectively filters aluminum while allowing sodium and other cations to pass through, demonstrating high efficiency in lab-scale experiments similar to real aluminum plant effluent.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights three key technologies reducing the aluminum industry's carbon footprint, including Hydro's HalZero process that eliminates CO2 emissions by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing impurities from recycled aluminum and the use of hydrogen as an alternative to natural gas in alumina refining to cut emissions.

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
summary: Recycling aluminum requires approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions, making it a critical material for sustainable vehicle manufacturing. The report underscores aluminum's role in improving efficiency and reducing environmental impact across all vehicle segments.

title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand
summary: Twenty-one million tons of additional steel production capacity has been announced or placed under construction in the past four years, signaling the most significant expansion of U.S. steelmaking capabilities in recent history. title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a globally sustainable battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the global nickel sector[1].

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, single-step method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon, cutting CO2 emissions by 84% and improving energy efficiency by 18%[2]. This breakthrough allows the processing of low-grade nickel ores, which account for 60% of total reserves, in a single reactor furnace to produce refined ferronickel directly[2].

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: Nickel is identified as a critical element for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, necessitating sustainable production scaling with minimal environmental impact[3]. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, highlighting the imperative for environmentally responsible production[3].

title: Clean energy's nickel rush is heading straight for some of Earth's richest ecosystems
summary: A new study warns that meeting future nickel demand for clean energy and stainless steel will require tough decisions that may threaten priority conservation areas in the tropics[4]. The research, published in Nature Ecology & Evolution, highlights the potential environmental conflicts arising from the surge in nickel mining needed for electric vehicle batteries and renewable energy equipment[4].

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: The global nickel market is undergoing rapid changes driven by innovations in mining and refining aimed at reducing production costs while enhancing environmental sustainability[8]. These developments focus on creating low-cost solutions that balance economic efficiency with the need for greener nickel production practices[8].
